title: Ink and Bone
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Great Library novel: in an alternate world where the Library of Alexandria never burned, owning a private book is a capital crime — and a young smuggler gets drafted into the Library's service.

title: Glass Houses
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Morganville Vampires novel: a brilliant teenager at a Texas college discovers the town is secretly run by vampires — and her roommates' protection may not be enough.

title: Working Stiff
author: Rachel Caine
description: The first Revivalist novel: a Dallas zombie reanimation specialist is murdered on the job and reanimates herself — and must survive long enough to solve her own killing.

title: Stillhouse Lake
author: Rachel Caine
description: The ex-wife of a convicted serial killer tries to build a new life for her kids in an isolated lake community — and discovers her past has followed her there.

title: Undead and Unwed
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: The first Betsy the Vampire Queen novel: a shoe-obsessed secretary is killed in a car accident, wakes up as a vampire, and discovers she is the prophesied queen of the undead.

title: Undead and Unemployed
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: Queen Betsy #2: Betsy tries to hold down a retail job at a Macy's designer shoe department while balancing her vampire queen duties and a serial killer targeting the undead.

title: Derik's Bane
author: MaryJanice Davidson
description: A werewolf enforcer of the Wyndham pack is sent to kill a mysterious woman in California — who turns out to be the most powerful witch on the West Coast.

title: Greywalker
author: Kat Richardson
description: The first Greywalker novel: a Seattle private investigator wakes from a two-minute death with the ability to walk in the Grey — the space between the living and the dead.

title: Poltergeist
author: Kat Richardson
description: Greywalker #2: Harper Blaine is hired to investigate a fraudulent poltergeist experiment at a Seattle university — and discovers it may be creating a real one.

title: Underground
author: Kat Richardson
description: Greywalker #3: a rash of killings in Seattle's homeless community leads Harper into the city's buried underground — and its old, angry ghosts.

title: Touch the Dark
author: Karen Chance
description: The first Cassandra Palmer novel: a clairvoyant raised by the vampire mafia goes on the run when her powers attract attention from vampires, the Pythia, and a nasty mage.

title: Claimed by Shadow
author: Karen Chance
description: Cassandra Palmer #2: Cassie must figure out her new role as Pythia — chief oracle of the supernatural world — while three men vie for her formal binding.

title: Midnight's Daughter
author: Karen Chance
description: The first Dorina Basarab novel: Dracula's daughter, a half-vampire bounty hunter, is hired to recover her father who has escaped confinement in the supernatural underworld.

title: Rosemary's Baby
author: Ira Levin
description: Levin's landmark occult horror: a young woman in 1960s Manhattan moves into an old apartment building, becomes pregnant, and slowly realizes her neighbors have plans for her child.

title: The Stepford Wives
author: Ira Levin
description: A woman moving with her husband to a picture-perfect Connecticut suburb realizes the local housewives are just a little too happy to cook and clean — and a little too blank.

title: A Kiss Before Dying
author: Ira Levin
description: Levin's debut: a young college man has plans to marry into money — and will do anything, to any woman, to get there. The first novel to win the Edgar Award for Best First Novel.

title: The Boys from Brazil
author: Ira Levin
description: A Nazi hunter discovers a former SS doctor in Paraguay is coordinating the murders of 94 65-year-old men across Europe — and uncovers the real reason why.

title: The Other
author: Thomas Tryon
description: Tryon's novel of twin boys on a 1935 Connecticut farm whose summer is haunted by a series of accidents — and whose dead mother may be speaking through one of them.

title: Harvest Home
author: Thomas Tryon
description: A Manhattan couple moves to the perfect New England village of Cornwall Coombe — and discovers the town practices a pre-Christian fertility religion the matriarchs intend to continue.

title: The Exorcist
author: William Peter Blatty
description: Blatty's landmark horror: a Washington actress's 12-year-old daughter begins exhibiting symptoms no doctor can explain — and a Jesuit priest is called in to consider demonic possession.

title: Legion
author: William Peter Blatty
description: A sequel to The Exorcist: twelve years after Regan's exorcism, a series of ritualistic murders in Georgetown leads a detective to a psychiatric hospital — and to a patient who should be dead.

title: Suffer the Children
author: John Saul
description: Saul's debut: a New England town that once saw a brutal child-killing is now seeing it happen again — and a family's buried secret may be the key to stopping it.

title: Punish the Sinners
author: John Saul
description: Saul's Oregon small-town horror: a young priest arriving in a strict Catholic community discovers a pattern of teenage-girl suicides going back over a century.

title: The God Project
author: John Saul
description: Children in a small American town are dying of a strange wasting illness that resists every medical explanation — except one their parents refuse to believe.

title: The Family Fang
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Wilson's debut: the grown children of a famously eccentric performance-art couple come home to confront the people who grew them up as props in their art.

title: Nothing to See Here
author: Kevin Wilson
description: A woman is hired by her wealthy college roommate to care for her husband's twin children — who spontaneously combust when they get upset.

title: Now Is Not the Time to Panic
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Two outcast teenagers in a small Tennessee town in the summer of 1996 create a mysterious poster that accidentally triggers a nationwide panic.

title: Run for the Hills
author: Kevin Wilson
description: Wilson's latest: three half-siblings who have never met decide to track down their vanished father and drive across the country to find him.

title: Heroes Die
author: Matthew Stover
description: The first Acts of Caine novel: an Earth entertainment star whose fictional persona is an actual fantasy-world assassin must rescue his estranged wife from the same world he's been killing in for years.

title: Blade of Tyshalle
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #2: Hari Michaelson, crippled in the events of Heroes Die, navigates an Earth bureaucracy while his alter ego Caine's fantasy world faces genocidal invasion.

title: Caine Black Knife
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #3: Caine returns to the fantasy world of his breakout film to finally confront what he did on that original mission twenty years ago.

title: Caine's Law
author: Matthew Stover
description: Acts of Caine #4: the conclusion of Stover's grimdark saga as Caine must choose between his Earth family and the fantasy world that has become his conscience.

title: Taliesin
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: The first Pendragon Cycle novel: a Welsh bard and an Atlantean princess conceive the boy who will become Merlin — the grandfather of King Arthur.

title: Merlin
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: Pendragon Cycle #2: the life of Merlin from his Druid boyhood through his madness and the raising of Arthur — Lawhead's Celtic-historical Arthurian.

title: Arthur
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: Pendragon Cycle #3: the reign of Arthur as told by three successive narrators — Pelleas, Bedwyr, and Aneirin — the most ambitious volume of the cycle.

title: Byzantium
author: Stephen R. Lawhead
description: An Irish monk carrying the Book of Kells to the Byzantine emperor is shipwrecked and sold into slavery — and his journey through the medieval world becomes a spiritual odyssey.

title: Green Rider
author: Kristen Britain
description: The first Green Rider novel: a schoolgirl fleeing expulsion encounters a dying king's messenger and inherits his mission to carry a critical message across the kingdom.

title: First Rider's Call
author: Kristen Britain
description: Green Rider #2: Karigan G'ladheon returns to Sacoridia and finds the magical wall that has protected the kingdom for centuries beginning to fail.

title: The High King's Tomb
author: Kristen Britain
description: Green Rider #3: Karigan confronts an awakening ancient evil while the kingdom is plagued by a series of magical thefts from its most sacred burial site.

title: Dragon Prince
author: Melanie Rawn
description: The first Dragon Prince novel: a young prince who refuses to hunt dragons inherits his throne and must navigate a political minefield to marry his prophesied consort.

title: The Star Scroll
author: Melanie Rawn
description: Dragon Prince #2: the discovery of a lost star scroll threatens to return ancient dangerous sorcery to the Desert kingdoms.

title: Sunrunner's Fire
author: Melanie Rawn
description: Dragon Prince #3: the conclusion of the original trilogy as Andry of Graypearl rises to claim his destiny as Lord of Goddess Keep.

title: Archangel
author: Sharon Shinn
description: The first Samaria novel: on a world where angels rule as God's intermediaries, the reluctant next Archangel must find his prophesied human bride among the slave caste.

title: Jovah's Angel
author: Sharon Shinn
description: Samaria #2: 150 years after Archangel, a hurt angel and an engineer investigate why the god Jovah no longer seems to answer the angels' songs.

title: The Alleluia Files
author: Sharon Shinn
description: Samaria #3: a group of heretics searches for ancient data files that may reveal the true nature of Jovah — and shatter the theocratic order of the angels.

title: Myrren's Gift
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The first Quickening trilogy: a young Morgravian general inherits a dying witch's strange gift and finds himself able to trade bodies with another man at will.

title: Blood and Memory
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The Quickening #2: Wyl Thirsk uses the witch's gift to survive assassination attempt after assassination attempt, each time waking in another body.

title: Bridge of Souls
author: Fiona McIntosh
description: The Quickening #3: the final confrontation with the king who betrayed Wyl and the reckoning with the witch's curse that has preserved him.

title: Night of Knives
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: The first Malazan Empire novel: set on the island of Malaz on the Shadow Moon night, a young initiate witnesses the event that will reshape the empire's leadership.

title: Return of the Crimson Guard
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: Malazan Empire #2: the ancient mercenary Crimson Guard return to Quon Tali after their century-long vow is fulfilled — and find the empire they vowed to destroy greatly changed.

title: Stonewielder
author: Ian Cameron Esslemont
description: Malazan Empire #3: on the Korel subcontinent, a long-isolated Malazan expedition must navigate the local gods' political crises and the return of the ancient Stormriders.

title: Hidden Empire
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: The first Saga of Seven Suns novel: humanity's stellar civilization accidentally ignites a gas giant — waking a species of beings who consider the act a declaration of war.

title: A Forest of Stars
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Saga of Seven Suns #2: the Hydrogues' war against organic life spreads across the galaxy as human, Ildiran, and tree-priest factions struggle to mount a response.

title: Horizon Storms
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Saga of Seven Suns #3: the war expands as other elemental races join the fight, and the secrets of the ancient Klikiss robots begin to come out.

title: Climbing Olympus
author: Kevin J. Anderson
description: Anderson's Mars-colonization novel: an 'augie' — a bioengineered settler — climbs Olympus Mons in search of the mystery that has been killing her kind.

title: The Summoner
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: The first Chronicles of the Necromancer: a young prince with the gift of speaking to the dead must flee his murdering brother and raise an army of spirits against him.

title: The Blood King
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: Chronicles of the Necromancer #2: Prince Tris Drayke gathers allies among the dead as his evil brother Jared consolidates power and courts a vampire ally.

title: Dark Haven
author: Gail Z. Martin
description: Chronicles of the Necromancer #3: King Martris Drayke rebuilds his war-torn kingdom while the last of Jared's magic reaches for him from the grave.

title: Hospital Station
author: James White
description: The first Sector General novel: linked stories of a gigantic multispecies hospital in deep space where the medical staff must learn to treat patients whose biology they've never seen.

title: Star Surgeon
author: James White
description: Sector General #2: Dr. Conway must handle a new crisis on a plague-planet while the hospital itself becomes the target of a military strike.

title: Major Operation
author: James White
description: Sector General #3: linked stories in which Dr. Conway investigates a world where the entire planet turns out to be a single vast organism.

title: Ventus
author: Karl Schroeder
description: A planet with terraforming AIs that have gone sentient and begun worshipping the long-dead human engineers who built them — Schroeder's transhuman SF debut.

title: Permanence
author: Karl Schroeder
description: An orphan pilot on a dead backwater colony stumbles into a mystery involving an ancient galactic civilization whose successors still walk among humanity.

title: Sun of Suns
author: Karl Schroeder
description: The first Virga novel: Schroeder's space opera set inside a giant balloon-world where towns rotate for gravity and nations wage wars between artificial suns.

title: Inkheart
author: Cornelia Funke
description: The first Inkworld novel: a father with the power to read characters out of their books accidentally brings a villain into our world — and loses his wife into the villain's book.

title: Inkspell
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Inkworld #2: Meggie and her father Mo are drawn into the Inkworld itself — the book whose fictional characters they've been freeing.

title: Inkdeath
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Inkworld #3: trapped inside the Inkworld as the evil Adderhead ravages the land, Meggie's family must write a new ending for a story that has become real.

title: The Thief Lord
author: Cornelia Funke
description: Two runaway brothers hiding in Venice fall in with a charismatic teenage thief who calls himself the Thief Lord — and who has a magical secret.

title: The Alchemyst
author: Michael Scott
description: The first Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel: teenage twins discover their bookseller employer is the immortal Nicholas Flamel, and they are the prophesied twins of legend.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: The Magician
author: Michael Scott
description: Nicholas Flamel #2: the twins travel to Paris to meet Niccolò Machiavelli and the Comte de Saint-Germain while Dr. John Dee hunts them across time.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: The Sorceress
author: Michael Scott
description: Nicholas Flamel #3: the twins travel to London to find the sorceress Gilgamesh and unlock the Book of Abraham's final secrets before Dee can destroy them.

series.name: The Secrets of the Immortal Nicholas Flamel

title: Mortal Engines
author: Philip Reeve
description: The first Mortal Engines novel: in a post-apocalyptic world of traction cities that roll across the land consuming smaller settlements, an apprentice historian falls out of London.

title: Predator's Gold
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #2: Tom and Hester flee to the ice-skating predator city of Anchorage as the Green Storm continues its war against the Traction Cities.

title: Infernal Devices
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #3: fifteen years later, Tom and Hester's daughter Wren is kidnapped by Lost Boys — and the fate of the Anchorage community is decided.

title: A Darkling Plain
author: Philip Reeve
description: Mortal Engines #4: the final confrontation with the Stalker Fang as the Traction Cities and Green Storm are both destroyed by a returning ancient weapon.

title: The Ear, the Eye, and the Arm
author: Nancy Farmer
description: In a 2194 Zimbabwe, three children of the military general are kidnapped and hunted by three mutant detectives with uncannily sharpened senses.

title: The House of the Scorpion
author: Nancy Farmer
description: In the future country of Opium, a boy raised for his organs realizes he is the clone of the 140-year-old drug lord El Patrón — and decides not to die for him.

title: A Girl Named Disaster
author: Nancy Farmer
description: An 11-year-old Mozambican girl flees an arranged marriage and crosses the African wilderness alone — Farmer's Newbery Honor coming-of-age adventure.

title: Winterlong
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: Hand's debut: in a post-apocalyptic Washington DC ruled by decadent aesthetes, a young empath is drawn into a plot that could bring back the old gods.

title: Waking the Moon
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: A young woman at a Catholic university in the 1970s stumbles onto an ancient moon-goddess cult whose members are gathering for an astrological ritual twenty years later.

title: Generation Loss
author: Elizabeth Hand
description: The first Cass Neary novel: a washed-out 1970s punk photographer travels to coastal Maine to interview a reclusive female art photographer — and walks into a disappearance.

title: Lost Souls
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: Brite's debut: three decadent vampires on a road trip through the American South encounter a young runaway with his own supernatural heritage.

title: Drawing Blood
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: A comic-book artist returning to his hometown and a hacker on the run from the FBI find themselves trapped in a haunted house at the edge of the Missing Mile.

title: Exquisite Corpse
author: Poppy Z. Brite
description: Two serial killers — one English, one American — find each other through a personals ad in New Orleans and become grotesquely in love.

title: The Priest of Blood
author: Douglas Clegg
description: The first Vampyricon novel: a medieval peasant huntsman is taken by vampire lords and sent on a quest to find a prophesied relic across a dying Europe.

title: Neverland
author: Douglas Clegg
description: Clegg's debut: a group of cousins spending summer at their grandmother's Georgia island discover a dark magical world ruled by their strange younger cousin.

title: The Halloween Man
author: Douglas Clegg
description: A woman returns to her hometown to confront the urban legend of a man who takes children on Halloween — and who may be more than a legend.

title: The Isle of Glass
author: Judith Tarr
description: The first Hound and the Falcon novel: a young half-elven monk in a medieval English monastery must choose between his vows and the elvenkind who claim him.

title: The Hall of the Mountain King
author: Judith Tarr
description: The first Avaryan Rising novel: a crown prince of a god-descended line must navigate political intrigue and magical destiny in Tarr's Byzantine-inspired fantasy.

title: A Fall of Princes
author: Judith Tarr
description: Avaryan Rising #3: the consolidation of the Sun-Son's empire through the eyes of an enemy prince — Tarr's most politically intricate volume.

title: So You Want to Be a Wizard
author: Diane Duane
description: The first Young Wizards novel: a bullied 13-year-old picks up a library book that is actually a wizarding manual and chooses to take the Oath.

title: Deep Wizardry
author: Diane Duane
description: Young Wizards #2: Nita and Kit join a group of cetacean wizards to perform a ritual that will save the coast — and that may require a human sacrifice.

title: High Wizardry
author: Diane Duane
description: Young Wizards #3: Nita's little sister Dairine receives her own wizard's manual and takes a wild interplanetary tour while Nita and Kit race to catch up.

title: The Door into Fire
author: Diane Duane
description: The first Tale of the Five novel: a young prince who can summon fire elementals searches across the land for his missing lover — Duane's adult fantasy debut.

title: Untouched by Human Hands
author: Robert Sheckley
description: Sheckley's first story collection: thirteen comic-absurdist SF tales that helped define the style of the early Galaxy magazine era.

title: Mindswap
author: Robert Sheckley
description: A man renting an alien body for a vacation is left stranded when his own body is stolen — and he must body-hop across the galaxy trying to get it back.

title: Dimension of Miracles
author: Robert Sheckley
description: A man who has won an interstellar prize he was never eligible for must find his way home through a series of increasingly absurd parallel universes.

title: Immortality, Inc.
author: Robert Sheckley
description: A 20th-century American is killed in a car crash and wakes up in the 22nd century in a body bought for him by a company that has just changed its mind.

title: Little Fuzzy
author: H. Beam Piper
description: A prospector on an alien colony world discovers a small primate-like creature — and must prove in court that it is sentient before his employer can have the species reclassified.

title: Space Viking
author: H. Beam Piper
description: In the post-Federation galaxy, a nobleman turned pirate-king uses his fleet to hunt the murderer of his wife across the fractured worlds of humanity.

title: Lord Kalvan of Otherwhen
author: H. Beam Piper
description: A Pennsylvania state trooper is transported to a parallel world with 17th-century technology and becomes the king who introduces them to modern firearms.

title: Agent of Vega
author: James H. Schmitz
description: The first Vegan Confederacy collection: linked stories of the elite Agents who keep order across a far-future galactic federation using psychic powers and deep cover.

title: The Witches of Karres
author: James H. Schmitz
description: A freighter captain rescues three children who turn out to be witches from the planet Karres — and finds himself on a chaotic adventure across the galaxy.

title: The Universe Against Her
author: James H. Schmitz
description: The first Telzey Amberdon novel: a telepathic teenager navigates the political machinery of the Federation while pursuing a secret mission.

title: Helix
author: Eric Brown
description: A starship crash-lands on a colossal artificial helix winding around a sun — a structure containing thousands of habitats occupied by different intelligent species.

title: New York Nights
author: Eric Brown
description: A New York private detective in 2040 investigates cases at the intersection of virtual reality and supernatural horror — Brown's noir cyberpunk novel.

title: Jani and the Greater Game
author: Eric Brown
description: An alternate-history Imperial Britain in 1925 where Empire rules via alien technology — and a young woman discovers the aliens have their own agenda for India.

title: Eclipse
author: John Shirley
description: The first A Song Called Youth novel: in near-future Europe, a fascist corporate army called the Second Alliance conquers a fractured continent as underground resistance begins.

title: Eclipse Penumbra
author: John Shirley
description: A Song Called Youth #2: the war against the Second Alliance expands as the resistance learns the full extent of the fascist corporate mind-control program.

title: Eclipse Corona
author: John Shirley
description: A Song Called Youth #3: the final battle between the resistance and the Second Alliance as humanity's fate on the Grid is decided.

title: The Shore of Women
author: Pamela Sargent
description: Centuries after a nuclear war, women live in high-tech walled cities while men live as primitive hunter-gatherers outside — until one woman is exiled and meets the 'savages.'

title: Cloned Lives
author: Pamela Sargent
description: Sargent's early novel: the first human clones — five siblings engineered from a single donor — grow up under public scrutiny and make very different lives.

title: Venus of Dreams
author: Pamela Sargent
description: The first Venus trilogy novel: multi-generational saga of a woman whose dream of terraforming Venus becomes the obsessive project of an entire political movement.

title: A Door into Ocean
author: Joan Slonczewski
description: On the water-world Shora, a race of all-female pacifist natives resist invasion from a patriarchal interstellar empire — Slonczewski's landmark feminist ecological SF.

title: The Children Star
author: Joan Slonczewski
description: Elysium Cycle: a missionary couple tries to adapt genetically modified human children to a lethal alien biosphere where microscopic life is sentient.

title: Brain Plague
author: Joan Slonczewski
description: Elysium Cycle: a painter allows an intelligent microbial colony to take up residence in her brain — and discovers an entire civilization of tiny beings in her head.

title: The House on the Borderland
author: William Hope Hodgson
description: Hodgson's cosmic horror masterwork: two travelers in rural Ireland discover a journal left in a ruined house — a manuscript of attack by pig-creatures and a vision of Earth's far future.

title: The Night Land
author: William Hope Hodgson
description: Hodgson's far-future epic: billions of years from now, the last humans live in a colossal pyramid-city surrounded by a dying Earth haunted by things the sun cannot reach.

title: The Ghost Pirates
author: William Hope Hodgson
description: A sailor signs on to a merchant ship and discovers the crew's growing certainty that they are being haunted — and then hunted — by spectral pirates from beyond the grave.

title: Carnacki the Ghost-Finder
author: William Hope Hodgson
description: Hodgson's occult-detective story collection: Thomas Carnacki investigates supernatural cases using a mixture of scientific instruments and pentacle-based protection.

title: The King in Yellow
author: Robert W. Chambers
description: Chambers's classic horror collection linked by a fictional play whose second act drives readers mad — a foundational weird fiction text influencing Lovecraft and True Detective.

title: The Maker of Moons
author: Robert W. Chambers
description: Chambers's second horror collection: stories of Chinese sorcery, occult conspiracies, and the strange creatures who appear when the moon is wrong.

title: The Collected Ghost Stories of E.F. Benson
author: E.F. Benson
description: Benson's complete supernatural fiction: Edwardian ghost stories and horror tales by one of the masters of the M.R. James school, collected in one volume.

title: The Room in the Tower
author: E.F. Benson
description: Benson's first horror collection, including the title story of a recurring nightmare that becomes real — one of the most-anthologized ghost stories in English.

title: Cold Hand in Mine
author: Robert Aickman
description: Aickman's World Fantasy Award-winning collection: eight 'strange stories' in which ordinary lives slide sideways into something quietly, profoundly wrong.

title: Dark Entries
author: Robert Aickman
description: Aickman's first solo collection: six strange stories of haunting, obsession, and loss that refuse to resolve into conventional ghost-story explanations.

title: Painted Devils
author: Robert Aickman
description: Aickman's sixth collection: strange stories of obsession, fate, and the thin membrane between the ordinary and the inexplicable.

title: World War Z
author: Max Brooks
description: An oral history of a global zombie outbreak told after the war's end by survivors from every continent and walk of life — Brooks's landmark pandemic novel.

title: The Zombie Survival Guide
author: Max Brooks
description: Brooks's fake-straight-face survival manual for a zombie apocalypse — the book that essentially created the modern zombie-preparation subgenre.

title: Devolution
author: Max Brooks
description: An eco-village in the Washington Cascades is cut off from the outside world by a Mount Rainier eruption — and learns it is not alone in the woods.

title: Six Stories
author: Matt Wesolowski
description: The first Six Stories novel: a true-crime podcaster investigates the twenty-year-old death of a teenager at an isolated outdoor center through six interviews with witnesses.

title: Hydra
author: Matt Wesolowski
description: Six Stories #2: the podcaster investigates the case of a woman who killed her entire family in a remote farmhouse — and whose defense involved a creature she called Hydra.

title: Beast
author: Matt Wesolowski
description: Six Stories #3: the investigation of three teenage YouTubers who livestreamed their deaths in a deserted tower block — and whose assailants have never been found.

title: The Keeper
author: Sarah Langan
description: Langan's debut: a dying Maine mill town is haunted by the return of a local woman whose drowning ten years earlier was never solved — and who has waited patiently for revenge.

title: The Missing
author: Sarah Langan
description: A sequel to The Keeper: Maine's Corpus Dei plague is spreading, and a small-town doctor must confront the growing horde of undead overtaking New England.

title: Good Neighbors
author: Sarah Langan
description: A suburban Long Island neighborhood's obsessive scapegoating of a single mother escalates into tragedy — Langan's social-horror novel of cul-de-sac cruelty.

title: Familiar Spirit
author: Lisa Tuttle
description: A woman who has recently moved to Texas is haunted by the jealous spirit of her rental house's previous tenant — Tuttle's contemporary psychological horror novel.

title: The Pillow Friend
author: Lisa Tuttle
description: A woman given a wish-granting doll as a child grows up discovering the costs of each of her childhood wishes as they come due one by one.

title: The Silver Bough
author: Lisa Tuttle
description: Three women find themselves trapped in a small Scottish town where an ancient fertility-magic custom is reasserting itself.

title: The Book of M
author: Peng Shepherd
description: An unexplained global phenomenon causes people to lose their shadows — and then their memories — and a couple must survive as one of them begins to forget.

title: The Cartographers
author: Peng Shepherd
description: A young woman investigates her cartographer father's death and uncovers a conspiracy centered on a mass-produced gas-station map that is hiding a secret town.

title: The Future Library
author: Peng Shepherd
description: Shepherd's novella for the Future Library project: a speculation about a library whose books will not be published for 100 years.

title: Dragon Bound
author: Thea Harrison
description: The first Elder Races novel: a half-fae thief steals from a dragon shapeshifter and finds her punishment is an arranged partnership with the monstrous creature she robbed.

title: Storm's Heart
author: Thea Harrison
description: Elder Races #2: a Wyr princess of a conspired-against thunderbird clan flees to Las Vegas and finds refuge with a dark fae assassin.

title: Serpent's Kiss
author: Thea Harrison
description: Elder Races #3: a healer Wyr is hired to save a comatose vampire and finds the case drawing her into ancient vampire politics.

title: Halfway to the Grave
author: Jeaniene Frost
description: The first Night Huntress novel: a half-vampire woman hunting vampires for revenge reluctantly teams up with an irresistible vampire bounty hunter.

title: One Foot in the Grave
author: Jeaniene Frost
description: Night Huntress #2: Cat works for a government vampire-hunting unit and is drawn back to her old partner Bones — and his enemies.

title: At Grave's End
author: Jeaniene Frost
description: Night Huntress #3: Cat and Bones's open relationship is threatened when an ancient vampire Cat's father once crossed returns to claim her.

title: Radiance
author: Grace Draven
description: The first Wraith Kings novel: a political marriage between a plain human noblewoman and a scholarly gray-skinned Kai prince becomes unexpectedly tender.

title: Eidolon
author: Grace Draven
description: Wraith Kings #2: Ildiko and Brishen must defend their kingdoms from an ancient evil bleeding across the borders between realms.

title: The Ippos King
author: Grace Draven
description: Wraith Kings #3: an ex-slave and a Kai warrior forge an unlikely alliance against a demonic threat and discover an unexpected bond.

title: Dealing with Dragons
author: Patricia C. Wrede
description: The first Enchanted Forest Chronicles novel: a princess tired of princess lessons runs away to become housekeeper to a dragon — and discovers dragons have problems of their own.

title: Searching for Dragons
author: Patricia C. Wrede
description: Enchanted Forest #2: the King of the Enchanted Forest and Princess Cimorene search for a missing dragon while the wizards continue their attacks on the forest.

title: Calling on Dragons
author: Patricia C. Wrede
description: Enchanted Forest #3: the witch Morwen's cats help Queen Cimorene investigate a plot involving the King's sword and an old dragon-wizard alliance.

title: Talking to Dragons
author: Patricia C. Wrede
description: Enchanted Forest #4 (and chronologically first-written): Cimorene's teenage son Daystar sets out into the Enchanted Forest on a quest to rescue his father.

title: The Misenchanted Sword
author: Lawrence Watt-Evans
description: The first Ethshar novel: a deserting soldier receives a magically enchanted sword whose curse is that he cannot die while holding it and cannot lose it for long.

title: With a Single Spell
author: Lawrence Watt-Evans
description: Ethshar #2: an apprentice wizard who only knows one minor spell must use his wit — and his single enchantment — to survive an increasingly dangerous quest.

title: The Unwilling Warlord
author: Lawrence Watt-Evans
description: Ethshar #3: a reluctant warlock is kidnapped and installed as the military ruler of a fractious city-state on the edge of destruction.

title: The Dragon Waiting
author: John M. Ford
description: Ford's World Fantasy Award-winning alternate history: an alternate 15th-century Europe in which Byzantium never fell, vampires exist, and Richard III may win the throne.

title: Growing Up Weightless
author: John M. Ford
description: A young boy born on a lunar colony tries to run away from his famous father — and in the process discovers what it means to be the second native-born generation of the Moon.

title: The Last Hot Time
author: John M. Ford
description: A young paramedic arrives in an urban-fantasy Chicago where elves, magic, and mob politics share the same nightclubs as 1940s jazz.

title: Imaro
author: Charles R. Saunders
description: Saunders's foundational work of sword-and-soul: a young warrior exiled from his cattle-herding people wanders a pre-colonial Africa-inspired continent called Nyumbani.

title: The Quest for Cush
author: Charles R. Saunders
description: Imaro #2: the exiled warrior journeys through the lands of Nyumbani seeking the ancient city of Cush and the truth of his origin.

title: The Trail of Bohu
author: Charles R. Saunders
description: Imaro #3: Imaro follows the trail of his sworn enemy Bohu into lands ruled by powerful sorcerer-priests — the original third volume of the Imaro saga.

title: I'm Waiting for You
author: Kim Bo-Young
description: A Korean SF novella in two parts: two lovers separated by relativistic travel exchange letters across time as they try to arrive together for their wedding.

title: On the Origin of Species and Other Stories
author: Kim Bo-Young
description: Kim's first English-translated story collection: seven tales spanning transhumanism, Korean folk-religion, and the far future of consciousness.

title: The Prophet of Corruption
author: Kim Bo-Young
description: Kim's Buddhist-inspired novel of reincarnation across many worlds — and a prophet who turns against the system of rebirth itself.

title: A College of Magics
author: Caroline Stevermer
description: A young woman arrives at a Victorian-era girls' college where the curriculum secretly includes sorcery — and discovers she is at the center of a political prophecy.

title: Sorcery and Cecelia
author: Caroline Stevermer
description: Stevermer and Patricia C. Wrede's epistolary Regency fantasy: two cousins exchange letters about the strange magical goings-on in their respective London and country seasons.

title: A Scholar of Magics
author: Caroline Stevermer
description: A College of Magics #2: an American sharpshooter is hired by an Oxford scholar to protect him during a dangerous magical experiment.

title: Nice Girls Don't Have Fangs
author: Molly Harper
description: The first Jane Jameson novel: a laid-off Kentucky children's librarian is turned into a vampire and must navigate her new eternal life and her mother's disapproval.

title: Nice Girls Don't Date Dead Men
author: Molly Harper
description: Jane Jameson #2: Jane's sister gets engaged to a werewolf, vampire sorority politics escalate, and Jane's boyfriend may be hiding a dark secret.

title: Nice Girls Don't Live Forever
author: Molly Harper
description: Jane Jameson #3: Jane's beloved vampire boyfriend Gabriel has a mysterious visit from his undead sire — who may or may not have plans to kill her.

title: Morlock Night
author: K.W. Jeter
description: Jeter's cult novel that coined the term 'steampunk' in a letter to Locus a decade later: a direct sequel to H.G. Wells's Time Machine in which the Morlocks invade Victorian London.

title: Infernal Devices
author: K.W. Jeter
description: Jeter's steampunk classic: a Victorian clockmaker inherits his father's mysterious device and is drawn into a secret war between automatons and fish-men.

title: Dr. Adder
author: K.W. Jeter
description: Jeter's legendary cyberpunk novel, so extreme it couldn't find a publisher for a decade: a far-future Los Angeles of augmented sex workers, terror TV, and bioweapons.

title: Noir
author: K.W. Jeter
description: A corporate retrieval agent investigating a bookshop murder in a cyberpunk future discovers a connection to an exiled philosopher's stolen data.

title: The Digging Leviathan
author: James P. Blaylock
description: In 1960s Los Angeles, a group of eccentrics follows an aging scientist's plans to build a submarine and dig to the hollow center of the Earth — Blaylock's dreamlike Southern California fantasy.

title: Homunculus
author: James P. Blaylock
description: Blaylock's Victorian steampunk: a derelict airship has been circling London for fifteen years, a homunculus is on the loose, and a cabal of occultists wants both.

title: The Last Coin
author: James P. Blaylock
description: The thirty pieces of silver Judas was paid for betraying Christ are scattered around the world — and a California beachcomber has the last one.

title: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind, Vol. 1
author: Hayao Miyazaki
description: The first volume of Miyazaki's post-apocalyptic manga epic: a young princess tries to make peace between warring human kingdoms and the mutated insect-haunted Sea of Corruption.

title: Starting Point
author: Hayao Miyazaki
description: Miyazaki's essays, interviews, and production notes from his first three decades at Studio Ghibli — the most direct window on his creative process.

title: The Keep
author: F. Paul Wilson
description: A Nazi garrison in WWII Romania takes over an ancient keep — and begins dying one by one as something that has been sealed inside the building for centuries wakes up.

title: The Tomb
author: F. Paul Wilson
description: The first Repairman Jack novel: an off-the-grid problem-solver-for-hire in New York takes a case involving missing relatives — and discovers ancient Hindu demons are hunting them.

title: Legacies
author: F. Paul Wilson
description: Repairman Jack #2: Jack investigates the deaths of a stage magician, his daughter, and a mysterious benefactor whose inheritance is tied to a lost Edison invention.

title: Sibs
author: F. Paul Wilson
description: A woman investigating her twin sister's suicide in New York uncovers a series of eerie deaths that may be tied to an otherworldly force that feeds on grief.

title: Reluctant Immortals
author: Gwendolyn Kiste
description: In 1967 Haight-Ashbury, two immortal women — one Lucy Westenra from Dracula, one Bertha from Jane Eyre — confront their monstrous male 'authors' who have come back to claim them.

title: The Rust Maidens
author: Gwendolyn Kiste
description: In 1980 Cleveland, a teenage girl watches her friends — known as the Rust Maidens — slowly transform into something that is shedding layers of themselves like rust.

title: Boneset & Feathers
author: Gwendolyn Kiste
description: A witch living in seclusion after escaping a brutal witch trial must confront her past when the witch hunters return to her village.

title: Nophek Gloss
author: Essa Hansen
description: The first Graven novel: a 14-year-old boy raised on a rural pastoral world discovers his entire life has been a deliberate illusion — and embarks on a revenge that spans multiverses.

title: Azura Ghost
author: Essa Hansen
description: The Graven #2: Caiden pursues the Graven entity whose past intrusion into the multiverse is unraveling reality — and discovers he may have been marked to stop it since childhood.

title: Ethera Grave
author: Essa Hansen
description: The Graven #3: the final confrontation across a shattered multiverse as Caiden's mission to save all universes becomes an ethical question.

title: Planetfall
author: Emma Newman
description: The first Planetfall novel: a colony of humans on a distant world has lived for decades around a mysterious alien structure — and the arrival of a stranger threatens the lie the leaders have been hiding.

title: After Atlas
author: Emma Newman
description: Planetfall #2: on a near-future Earth where the Planetfall mission has been mythologized, a famous detective is hired to investigate the death of a cult leader.

title: Between Two Thorns
author: Emma Newman
description: The first Split Worlds novel: a young woman in an alternate Victorian England caught between fae politics and magical obligations she did not choose.

title: Before Mars
author: Emma Newman
description: Planetfall #3: a geologist arriving at the Mars colony finds a note written in her own handwriting warning her not to trust the psychiatrist on base.

title: Machinehood
author: S.B. Divya
description: A 2095 bodyguard for a pharmaceutical pill-designer investigates a terrorist group called the Machinehood that wants to pause all human work — and may be partly nonhuman.

title: Meru
author: S.B. Divya
description: The first Alloy Era novel: an engineered post-human girl and a traditional-genetics pilot team up to investigate the first truly habitable human-compatible planet discovered in centuries.

title: Loka
author: S.B. Divya
description: The Alloy Era #2: Akshaya's daughter attempts to live as a regular human on a post-scarcity Earth and finds herself shaping the planet's political future.

title: Truthwitch
author: Susan Dennard
description: The first Witchlands novel: two witch friends — one a rare Truthwitch, one a Threadwitch — flee across a war-torn continent as empires and assassins hunt them.

title: Windwitch
author: Susan Dennard
description: The Witchlands #2: prince Merik fakes his own death while Safi is held prisoner by the Hell-Bards — and a new enemy begins to stir in the Origin Wells.

title: Bloodwitch
author: Susan Dennard
description: The Witchlands #3: Aeduan, the titular Bloodwitch, and the truthwitch-in-training Iseult must confront the ancient curse that binds them both.

title: Something Strange and Deadly
author: Susan Dennard
description: Dennard's debut: in 1876 Philadelphia, a young woman whose brother has gone missing investigates rumors of a necromancer-led zombie uprising at the Centennial Exposition.

title: The Kiss of Deception
author: Mary E. Pearson
description: The first Remnant Chronicles novel: a runaway princess is pursued by an assassin and a prince of a rival kingdom — both of whom reach her incognito in the same village.

title: The Heart of Betrayal
author: Mary E. Pearson
description: Remnant Chronicles #2: Lia is held captive by the Komizar of Venda and must find a way to survive long enough to prevent a continent-wide war.

title: The Beauty of Darkness
author: Mary E. Pearson
description: Remnant Chronicles #3: the final confrontation with the Komizar as Lia rallies her kingdoms to confront the gathered Vendan horde.

title: Dance of Thieves
author: Mary E. Pearson
description: Set in the Remnant Chronicles world: a young outlaw queen and a rahtan agent investigating her outpost fall for each other — and learn they are on opposite sides of a coming war.

title: A Dance of Cloaks
author: David Dalglish
description: The first Shadowdance novel: the son of a powerful crime-guild leader is raised to become the perfect assassin — and discovers his empathy may be his only advantage.

title: A Dance of Blades
author: David Dalglish
description: Shadowdance #2: Haern the Watcher, a vigilante assassin, is hired to disrupt a war that is about to engulf Veldaren — and finds the politics are more complex than he realized.

title: Soulkeeper
author: David Dalglish
description: The first Keepers novel: a church soulkeeper — a priest trained to shepherd dying souls to their next journey — confronts a wave of impossible awakenings.

title: The Novice
author: Taran Matharu
description: The first Summoner novel: a young blacksmith's son discovers he has a rare talent for summoning demons and is recruited into the Empire's elite Vocans Academy.

title: The Inquisition
author: Taran Matharu
description: Summoner #2: Fletcher stands trial for his mysterious background and heritage — while the orc invasion grows closer to the empire's frontier.

title: The Battlemage
author: Taran Matharu
description: Summoner #3: Fletcher and his friends are thrown into the orc-infested ether and must find a way back before the Empire collapses under an orc assault.

title: The Unremembered
author: Peter Orullian
description: The first Vault of Heaven novel: a young orphan in a quiet village discovers his bloodline is at the heart of an ancient war between gods and an imprisoned evil.

title: Trial of Intentions
author: Peter Orullian
description: Vault of Heaven #2: the heroes prepare for the coming Quietgiven while political and theological factions fracture the kingdom they are trying to save.

title: The Eagle of the Ninth
author: Rosemary Sutcliff
description: Sutcliff's classic: a young Roman centurion and his British slave travel north of Hadrian's Wall to recover the lost Eagle of the Ninth Legion.

title: The Silver Branch
author: Rosemary Sutcliff
description: Dolphin Ring #2: a century after The Eagle of the Ninth, two descendants of Marcus Flavius Aquila defend Roman Britain against Saxon raiders.

title: The Lantern Bearers
author: Rosemary Sutcliff
description: Dolphin Ring #3: at the end of Roman rule in Britain, a young officer deserts the last legion and joins the British resistance against the Saxon settlers. Carnegie Medal winner.

title: The Mark of the Horse Lord
author: Rosemary Sutcliff
description: A Roman gladiator who has won his freedom is recruited to impersonate the long-lost king of the Dalriads — and must survive the Pictish war that greets his arrival.

title: A Madness of Angels
author: Kate Griffin
description: The first Matthew Swift novel: a London sorcerer dies in an alley and wakes up two years later, possessed by the blue electric angels of the telephone wires and hunting his own killer.

title: The Midnight Mayor
author: Kate Griffin
description: Matthew Swift #2: Matthew is drafted into the mystical role of the Midnight Mayor — London's supernatural protector — and must confront an ancient evil stirring in the Square Mile.

title: The Neon Court
author: Kate Griffin
description: Matthew Swift #3: the Neon Court of London's supernatural elites demands Matthew find a missing chosen one before a prophesied catastrophe.

title: Before I Fall
author: Lauren Oliver
description: Oliver's debut: a popular high school girl dies in a car accident — and relives the same day seven times, each time trying to figure out what she's meant to do differently.

title: Delirium
author: Lauren Oliver
description: The first Delirium novel: in a future where love has been declared a disease and surgically cured, a teenager falls for a rebel boy ninety-five days before her own scheduled cure.

title: Pandemonium
author: Lauren Oliver
description: Delirium #2: Lena survives in the Wilds while a growing resistance movement plots against the regime that cures love.

title: Requiem
author: Lauren Oliver
description: Delirium #3: Lena's life and the war against the regime collide as the rebels prepare for a final uprising.

title: Matched
author: Ally Condie
description: The first Matched novel: in a society where everyone is assigned a perfect partner at age 17, a young woman sees a second face flicker in her Match report.

title: Crossed
author: Ally Condie
description: Matched #2: Cassia has been sent to the Outer Provinces as punishment for her dissent — and joins a growing resistance against the Society's control.

title: Reached
author: Ally Condie
description: Matched #3: the final chapter in Cassia's rebellion against the Society as a plague begins to sweep across the Provinces.

title: Across the Universe
author: Beth Revis
description: The first Across the Universe novel: a teenage girl cryogenically frozen for a 300-year interstellar journey is woken 50 years early — and realizes someone is murdering the passengers.

title: A Million Suns
author: Beth Revis
description: Across the Universe #2: Amy and Elder must navigate the social collapse of the Godspeed as the truth about the ship's mission begins to unravel.

title: Shades of Earth
author: Beth Revis
description: Across the Universe #3: the Godspeed arrives at Centauri-Earth — and the survivors discover the planet has deadly secrets of its own.

title: I Am Not a Serial Killer
author: Dan Wells
description: A teenager with clinical sociopathy works at his mother's mortuary and spends all his energy not becoming a serial killer — until a real one arrives in town.

title: Mr. Monster
author: Dan Wells
description: John Cleaver #2: John has killed a demon, but his inner 'Mr. Monster' is getting stronger — and another supernatural killer is targeting young women in his town.

title: I Don't Want to Kill You
author: Dan Wells
description: John Cleaver #3: John deliberately calls out a new demon — and regrets it as it begins hunting his family and the people he cares about.

title: Partials
author: Dan Wells
description: The first Partials Sequence novel: in a 2076 America where only 40,000 humans survive after a war with the bioengineered Partials, a teenage medic tries to find a cure for infant mortality.

title: Sword Art Online: Aincrad
author: Reki Kawahara
description: The first Sword Art Online light novel: 10,000 players are trapped in a VR MMO that can only be exited by beating all 100 floors — and dying in-game kills you in reality.

title: Sword Art Online: Fairy Dance
author: Reki Kawahara
description: Sword Art Online #3-4: Kirito investigates a new VR game where Asuna is being held prisoner in a high-tech magical kingdom.

title: Accel World, Vol. 1
author: Reki Kawahara
description: The first Accel World light novel: a bullied middle-schooler is invited to join a hidden online game where the fastest combatants become Burst Linkers.

title: Finna
author: Nino Cipri
description: Two minimum-wage employees at a LitenVärld (not-IKEA) store use an interdimensional-wormhole-locator device to find a customer who wandered into another universe.

title: Defekt
author: Nino Cipri
description: LitenVärld #2: an Employee of the Month is assigned to an overnight shift and discovers his coworkers are all clones of himself — and something lives in the bedding aisle.

title: Homesick
author: Nino Cipri
description: Cipri's first collection: trans and non-binary characters navigating haunted houses, vanished worlds, and the slow unraveling of gendered certainties.

title: The Girls at the Kingfisher Club
author: Genevieve Valentine
description: A Jazz Age retelling of The Twelve Dancing Princesses: twelve sisters hidden by their abusive father escape nightly to Manhattan speakeasies — and must ultimately flee together.

title: Mechanique
author: Genevieve Valentine
description: Valentine's debut: in a post-apocalyptic world, a traveling circus whose aerialists are fitted with mechanical bones draws both devotees and inquisitors.

title: Persona
author: Genevieve Valentine
description: In a near-future world of celebrity diplomats, a face-for-hire is framed for the assassination of a rival Face — and must survive her handlers and the press.

title: The Invisibles, Vol. 1: Say You Want a Revolution
author: Grant Morrison
description: Morrison's psychedelic counterculture-conspiracy epic: a British cell of the Invisibles fights the Outer Church through time-bending reality and pop-magic warfare.

title: All-Star Superman, Vol. 1
author: Grant Morrison
description: Morrison and Frank Quitely's twelve-issue Superman saga: Superman's exposure to solar radiation gives him enough power to complete his twelve labors before he dies.

title: We3
author: Grant Morrison
description: Morrison and Frank Quitely: three animals — a dog, a cat, and a rabbit — are converted into weapons and escape their military handlers.

title: Flex Mentallo
author: Grant Morrison
description: Morrison and Frank Quitely's meta-fictional four-part series: a superhero-parody bodybuilder named Flex Mentallo searches for meaning at the intersection of comics and reality.

title: Transmetropolitan, Vol. 1: Back on the Street
author: Warren Ellis
description: Ellis's cyberpunk-gonzo: Spider Jerusalem, a Hunter S. Thompson-esque journalist, returns from reclusive exile to the City to investigate its new fascist president.

title: Planetary, Vol. 1: All Over the World
author: Warren Ellis
description: Ellis and John Cassaday's meta-comics: three field agents of the Planetary organization investigate the secret history of the 20th century across a world of forgotten pulp heroes.

title: The Authority, Vol. 1: Relentless
author: Warren Ellis
description: Ellis's superhero revamp: a team of post-human defenders drops the pretense of government partnership and starts treating superhuman problems as global politics.

title: Crooked Little Vein
author: Warren Ellis
description: Ellis's novel: a washed-up private investigator in New York is hired by the President's chief of staff to find a secret alternate Constitution — and is sent through the vilest underbelly of American culture.

title: Hellboy, Vol. 1: Seed of Destruction
author: Mike Mignola
description: The first Hellboy collection: a demon summoned to Earth by Nazis in 1944 is raised by the American military and grows up to fight the occult on behalf of the BPRD.

title: Hellboy, Vol. 2: Wake the Devil
author: Mike Mignola
description: Hellboy #2: Hellboy investigates a vampire resurrection in Romania and confronts his own prophesied destiny as the Beast of the Apocalypse.

title: Hellboy in Hell, Vol. 1: The Descent
author: Mike Mignola
description: The final Hellboy arc: after dying in The Storm and the Fury, Hellboy wakes up in Hell and begins an exploration of his true home — and his ancient inheritance.

title: Preacher, Vol. 1: Gone to Texas
author: Garth Ennis
description: Ennis and Steve Dillon's Texas preacher is possessed by the entity Genesis — offspring of an angel and a demon — and sets off with his ex-girlfriend and an Irish vampire to find God.

title: The Boys, Vol. 1: The Name of the Game
author: Garth Ennis
description: Ennis's cynical superhero deconstruction: a CIA-funded team of non-powered humans keeps watch on corporate superheroes — and kills them when they get out of line.

title: Punisher MAX, Vol. 1: In the Beginning
author: Garth Ennis
description: Ennis's definitive take on Frank Castle: a more realistic, brutal Punisher operating in a world without superheroes and without clean consciences.

title: Understanding Comics
author: Scott McCloud
description: McCloud's landmark comics-theory book, itself in comics form: an analytical tour of how sequential art works, why it's an art form unto itself, and how readers participate in it.

title: Reinventing Comics
author: Scott McCloud
description: McCloud's follow-up to Understanding Comics: the twelve revolutions he saw coming to comics — from digital publication to creator ownership and micropayments.

title: Making Comics
author: Scott McCloud
description: McCloud's how-to guide for aspiring comics creators: storytelling, pacing, character design, and the craft of sequential art — taught in comics form.

title: The Ruins of Gorlan
author: John Flanagan
description: The first Ranger's Apprentice novel: a small orphan boy is chosen by the mysterious Ranger Corps to serve as an apprentice to a hooded master-scout.

title: The Burning Bridge
author: John Flanagan
description: Ranger's Apprentice #2: Will and his mentor must uncover a treasonous plot while leading a diplomatic mission to the neighboring kingdom of Celtica.

title: The Icebound Land
author: John Flanagan
description: Ranger's Apprentice #3: Will and Evanlyn are enslaved by Skandian pirates and must survive a winter in the frozen north while Halt comes to rescue them.

title: The Battle for Skandia
author: John Flanagan
description: Ranger's Apprentice #4: Will and Halt return to Skandia to help the Oberjarl defend the kingdom from a massive Temujai invasion.

title: The Physiognomy
author: Jeffrey Ford
description: The first Well-Built City novel: an arrogant physiognomist investigates a series of murders in a strange imperial city and is exiled to a labor camp.

title: The Portrait of Mrs. Charbuque
author: Jeffrey Ford
description: In 19th-century Manhattan, a society portrait painter is hired to paint a wealthy woman who insists he cannot see her — only hear her life story through a screen.

title: The Girl in the Glass
author: Jeffrey Ford
description: A Depression-era spiritualism conman and his medium partner discover a real ghost during one of their staged séances — Ford's Edgar Award-winning novel.

title: Thunderer
author: Felix Gilman
description: Gilman's debut: in the massive city of Ararat, a thousand gods compete for worshippers and a new revolutionary movement tries to free the city from the theocrats.

title: Gears of the City
author: Felix Gilman
description: Ararat #2: an exiled character from Thunderer returns to discover the city has grown beyond his understanding, and new political factions are shaping its gods.

title: The Half-Made World
author: Felix Gilman
description: Gilman's western-fantasy: a psychiatrist from the civilized East travels into the Half-Made World — a still-being-created frontier ruled by supernatural Gun and Line factions.

title: The Strange Case of the Alchemist's Daughter
author: Theodora Goss
description: The first Athena Club novel: Mary Jekyll, Diana Hyde, Catherine Moreau, Justine Frankenstein, and Beatrice Rappaccini form a detective club in Victorian London.

series.name: The Extraordinary Adventures of the Athena Club

title: European Travel for the Monstrous Gentlewoman
author: Theodora Goss
description: The Athena Club #2: the Club travels to Vienna and Budapest to rescue Lucinda Van Helsing and confronts the alchemical society that created them all.

series.name: The Extraordinary Adventures of the Athena Club

title: The Sinister Mystery of the Mesmerizing Girl
author: Theodora Goss
description: The Athena Club #3: back in London, the Club investigates a kidnapping plot involving a young woman with mesmeric powers — and the Society of the Golden Dawn.

series.name: The Extraordinary Adventures of the Athena Club

title: Things in Jars
author: Jess Kidd
description: A Victorian lady detective in 1863 London investigates the kidnapping of a strange child being kept as a curiosity — and who may not be entirely human.

title: The Night Ship
author: Jess Kidd
description: Kidd's dual-timeline novel: a girl on the 17th-century Batavia shipwreck and a 1989 Australian boy whose island becomes the site of reckonings with a long-buried tragedy.

title: Himself
author: Jess Kidd
description: Kidd's debut: a charming hitman who sees ghosts returns to the Irish village where his mother was killed — and sets out to solve the 26-year-old mystery.

title: The Manitou
author: Graham Masterton
description: Masterton's horror debut: a Manhattan fortune teller's client grows a strange tumor on her neck — which is actually a Native American medicine man's reincarnated body.

title: Prey
author: Graham Masterton
description: An English architect renovating an Isle of Wight cottage is haunted by an entity that has been waiting in the house for centuries — and wants a body of its own.

title: Ritual
author: Graham Masterton
description: A New Orleans restaurant critic investigates a ritual cannibal cult whose members are butchering themselves at a gourmet chef's behest.

title: Chasing the Boogeyman
author: Richard Chizmar
description: Chizmar's fake-memoir novel: a small Maryland town in 1988 is stalked by a serial killer called the Boogeyman, and a young reporter investigates using his own childhood experiences.

title: Becoming the Boogeyman
author: Richard Chizmar
description: The sequel to Chasing the Boogeyman: Chizmar returns to Edgewood as a man connected to the original killings is released from prison.

title: Gwendy's Button Box
author: Richard Chizmar
description: Chizmar's collaboration with Stephen King: a 12-year-old girl in 1974 Castle Rock receives a mysterious button box from a mysterious man in a small top hat.

title: Tome of the Undergates
author: Sam Sykes
description: The first Aeons' Gate novel: a band of misfit adventurers is hired to recover a book that can unlock the gates of the Abysmyth — and of course they fail catastrophically.

title: Black Halo
author: Sam Sykes
description: Aeons' Gate #2: Lenk and his dysfunctional crew continue to argue about the book while the demons they were trying to contain escape and hunt them down.

title: The Mortal Tally
author: Sam Sykes
description: The second Bring Down Heaven novel: Lenk and his companions confront the return of the gods to a world that has learned to survive without them.

title: Wicked as They Come
author: Delilah S. Dawson
description: The first Blud novel: a woman transported to an alternate steampunk Victorian London falls for a charming vampire — and discovers the locket that brought her there is a key.

title: Hit
author: Delilah S. Dawson
description: A 17-year-old whose mother's medical debt is sold to Valor Savings Bank is drafted as one of its teenage assassins — and given a list of ten people to kill.

title: The Violence
author: Delilah S. Dawson
description: A pandemic that causes people to have brief blackouts during which they become extremely violent tears apart a Florida family and reveals their secrets.

title: The Forest of Hands and Teeth
author: Carrie Ryan
description: Ryan's debut: a teenage girl in a fenced village surrounded by a forest full of infected undead begins to question what her Sisterhood leaders have been hiding from her.

series.name: The Forest of Hands and Teeth

title: The Dead-Tossed Waves
author: Carrie Ryan
description: Forest of Hands and Teeth #2: a young woman in a post-zombie coastal settlement discovers her mother was the girl from the original book — and that the infection is changing.

series.name: The Forest of Hands and Teeth

title: The Dark and Hollow Places
author: Carrie Ryan
description: Forest of Hands and Teeth #3: the conclusion of Ryan's post-zombie trilogy as the last human settlement falls to the plague and the survivors begin a desperate exodus.

series.name: The Forest of Hands and Teeth

title: The Haunted Vagina
author: Carlton Mellick III
description: Mellick's cult bizarro novella: a woman's vagina is literally haunted, and her boyfriend must journey into her to confront whatever lives there.

title: Satan Burger
author: Carlton Mellick III
description: Mellick's debut: a group of punks in a dying city get jobs at a fast-food chain owned by Satan — and learn what humanity's last days look like.

title: The Baby Jesus Butt Plug
author: Carlton Mellick III
description: Mellick's satirical bizarro: a corporate drone becomes obsessed with a new consumer product and his office descends into an increasingly literal hell.

title: Beyond the Deepwoods
author: Paul Stewart
description: The first Edge Chronicles: a young orphan raised by woodtrolls sets out into the deep forest of the Edge to find his true parentage — and discovers a world of sky pirates and floating cities.

title: Stormchaser
author: Paul Stewart
description: Edge Chronicles #2: Twig, now an apprentice sky pirate, must sail into the heart of a Great Storm to recover a fragment of stormphrax that can save Sanctaphrax.

title: Midnight Over Sanctaphrax
author: Paul Stewart
description: Edge Chronicles #3: a Mother Storm is approaching Sanctaphrax, and Twig must find a way to save the floating city before it is ripped from its moorings.

title: A Shadow Bright and Burning
author: Jessica Cluess
description: The first Kingdom on Fire novel: a Victorian orphan with secret flame magic is recruited into a society of male sorcerers who believe she is the prophesied chosen one.

title: A Poison Dark and Drowning
author: Jessica Cluess
description: Kingdom on Fire #2: Henrietta Howel and her fellow sorcerers hunt the Ancients across England — and discover the true source of her fire magic.

title: A Sorrow Fierce and Falling
author: Jessica Cluess
description: Kingdom on Fire #3: the final confrontation with the demonic Ancients as Henrietta and her allies fight to save England and each other.

title: The Encyclopedia of Early Earth
author: Isabel Greenberg
description: Greenberg's debut: a mythology-textured graphic novel about a storyteller from the North Pole who travels the world trying to find a woman he cannot physically touch.

title: The One Hundred Nights of Hero
author: Isabel Greenberg
description: A graphic-novel retelling of the Thousand and One Nights, set in Greenberg's Early Earth universe: a woman and her wife tell stories for a hundred nights to survive a wager.

title: Glass Town
author: Isabel Greenberg
description: Greenberg's graphic-novel imagining of the Brontë siblings' shared imaginary world of Glass Town and the fictional characters who lived inside it.

title: Something Is Killing the Children, Vol. 1
author: James Tynion IV
description: Tynion and Werther Dell'Edera's horror comic: in the town of Archer's Peak, children are being killed by monsters only they can see — and a young woman called Erica Slaughter arrives to hunt them.

title: The Department of Truth, Vol. 1
author: James Tynion IV
description: Tynion and Martin Simmonds's conspiracy-horror: an FBI agent joins a secret government department responsible for keeping conspiracy theories from becoming true.

title: The Nice House on the Lake, Vol. 1
author: James Tynion IV
description: Tynion and Álvaro Martínez Bueno's horror comic: ten friends are invited by their mysterious mutual friend Walter to a lake house — and learn the world has ended without them.

series.name: The Nice House on the Lake

title: Where Loyalties Lie
author: Rob J. Hayes
description: The first Best Laid Plans novel: a pirate king with a dark past tries to unite the pirates of the Pirate Isles into a single fleet — before the three kingdoms destroy them all.

title: The Heresy Within
author: Rob J. Hayes
description: The first Ties That Bind novel: three characters — an aging inquisitor, a necromantic apprentice, and a mercenary captain — converge on a grimdark continent's darkest secret.

title: Never Die
author: Rob J. Hayes
description: Hayes's Asian-inspired standalone: a dying samurai is hired by a boy to gather five fallen heroes from across the land — and then kill a god.

title: Along the Razor's Edge
author: Rob J. Hayes
description: The first War Eternal novel: an enslaved girl with forbidden magical talent orchestrates a prison rebellion that will shape the rest of her life.

title: Paternus: Rise of Gods
author: Dyrk Ashton
description: The first Paternus Trilogy novel: every god and monster from every world mythology is real, and they are secretly fighting a centuries-long war hidden beneath modern Ohio.

title: Paternus: Wrath of Gods
author: Dyrk Ashton
description: Paternus Trilogy #2: the hidden gods and their chosen humans flee across the globe as the Firstborn tear away the last barriers between mythology and reality.

title: Paternus: War of Gods
author: Dyrk Ashton
description: Paternus Trilogy #3: the final confrontation between the hidden pantheon and the Asura — a war fought across the entire scope of human myth.

title: An Accident of Stars
author: Foz Meadows
description: The first Manifold Worlds novel: a troubled Australian teenager accidentally travels through a portal to a matriarchal polyamorous kingdom — and is drawn into its political crisis.

title: A Tyranny of Queens
author: Foz Meadows
description: Manifold Worlds #2: Saffron must adjust to life back on Earth while the empire she helped save in Kena tears itself apart.

title: A Strange and Stubborn Endurance
author: Foz Meadows
description: Meadows's m/m romance fantasy: a young noble diplomat marries into a rival kingdom's royal family — and must survive a web of courtly intrigue and personal trauma.

title: The Dead Lands
author: Benjamin Percy
description: Percy's post-apocalyptic western: 150 years after a pandemic and nuclear war, a survivor named Lewis leads an expedition out of walled St. Louis into the American interior.

title: Red Moon
author: Benjamin Percy
description: An alternate-world America where lycanthropy is a political identity: a teenage werewolf, a young woman with prosthetic legs, and an ambitious politician confront a looming war.

title: The Dark Net
author: Benjamin Percy
description: A 12-year-old blind girl who has just received experimental implants that restore her sight begins to see something else — a demonic force crawling out of the internet.

title: The Ninth Metal
author: Benjamin Percy
description: The first Comet Cycle novel: a meteor shower leaves a powerful new metal called 'omnimetal' embedded across Minnesota — and a mining boomtown is born around it.

title: Redwood and Wildfire
author: Andrea Hairston
description: Hairston's Tiptree-winning novel: a hoodoo conjure woman and a Seminole-Irish magic man travel from 1898 Georgia to Chicago and then to Hollywood — the early American entertainment industry through Black and Indigenous eyes.

title: Will Do Magic for Small Change
author: Andrea Hairston
description: In 1986 Pittsburgh, a teenage girl inherits a magical manuscript that tells the story of a shape-shifting extra-dimensional being traveling through 19th-century Dahomey.

title: Master of Poisons
author: Andrea Hairston
description: Hairston's ecological fantasy: in a dying African-inspired continent, a Master of Poisons travels with a young rebel to find the cause of the plague consuming the land.

title: The Prey of Gods
author: Nicky Drayden
description: Drayden's debut: a near-future South Africa where a drug turns the addicted into gods, a teen robotics prodigy, a pop-star politician, and a hidden entity all collide.

title: Temper
author: Nicky Drayden
description: In an alternate-Earth society where twins are ranked by the virtues and vices they were born with, a pair of brothers discovers their imbalance is part of an ancient war.

title: Escaping Exodus
author: Nicky Drayden
description: Humanity's survivors live inside massive bioengineered space creatures — and the young heir to a dying people must decide whether to defy her culture to save the beast they are killing.

title: Half a Soul
author: Olivia Atwater
description: The first Regency Faerie Tales novel: a young Englishwoman cursed by a faerie lord as a child has only half a soul — until a disreputable magician decides to help her reclaim it.

title: Ten Thousand Stitches
author: Olivia Atwater
description: Regency Faerie Tales #2: a maid in a Regency English household embroiders handkerchiefs to trap a faerie lord in a marriage bargain.

title: Longshadow
author: Olivia Atwater
description: Regency Faerie Tales #3: a young woman with faerie-tainted shadow magic investigates a series of deaths in Regency London and encounters both fae and men who would destroy her.

title: Ammonite
author: Nicola Griffith
description: Griffith's debut: an anthropologist is sent to a colony world where a virus killed all the men generations ago — and the women have developed their own unique civilization.

title: Slow River
author: Nicola Griffith
description: Griffith's Nebula-winning near-future novel: a corporate heiress, kidnapped and left for dead, builds a new identity working at a sewage-treatment plant in a Northern English city.

title: Hild
author: Nicola Griffith
description: Griffith's masterwork: the early life of Saint Hilda of Whitby, born in 614 AD to a dispossessed royal family and raised to serve her uncle the king of Northumbria as a seer.

title: Creatures of Will and Temper
author: Molly Tanzer
description: Tanzer's Victorian-inspired reimagining of The Picture of Dorian Gray: two sisters arriving in 1880s London are drawn into a secret society of demon-bonded aesthetes.

title: Creatures of Want and Ruin
author: Molly Tanzer
description: Diabolist's Library #2: in 1920s Long Island, a bootlegger discovers the liquor she's selling contains something that is making its drinkers murderous.

title: Vermilion
author: Molly Tanzer
description: A Chinese-American gunslinger and psychopomp in the 1870s American West tries to rescue missing Chinese immigrants from a mountain sanatorium run by something inhuman.

title: Dead Spots
author: Melissa F. Olson
description: The first Scarlett Bernard novel: a Los Angeles 'null' whose presence cancels supernatural abilities gets hired to clean up after supernatural crimes before the human police find them.

title: Trail of Dead
author: Melissa F. Olson
description: Scarlett Bernard #2: Scarlett's investigation of a series of supernatural murders leads her to Colorado and a coven of witches with their own agenda.

title: Hunter's Trail
author: Melissa F. Olson
description: Scarlett Bernard #3: Scarlett is hired to hunt a newly-made werewolf before the full moon — but as usual, nothing in the LA supernatural world is what it seems.

title: The Night Gardener
author: Jonathan Auxier
description: Two Irish orphans take jobs as servants at a Victorian English manor haunted by a mysterious night gardener — and by a tree that grants wishes at a terrible price.

title: Peter Nimble and His Fantastic Eyes
author: Jonathan Auxier
description: Auxier's debut: a blind ten-year-old thief receives a magical box containing three pairs of eyes and is recruited to save a missing kingdom.

title: Sweep
author: Jonathan Auxier
description: Auxier's tale of a Victorian chimney sweep girl and her golem companion — a creature born from ash — fighting the cruel master who owns them both.

title: A Tale Dark and Grimm
author: Adam Gidwitz
description: Gidwitz's retelling of the Grimm brothers' darkest fairy tales: Hansel and Gretel are the protagonists of eight very un-sanitized stories from the original collection.

title: The Inquisitor's Tale
author: Adam Gidwitz
description: Gidwitz's Newbery Honor medieval epic: a group of travelers at a French inn in 1242 tell the linked story of three magical children on the run from the Inquisition.

title: In a Glass Grimmly
author: Adam Gidwitz
description: A Tale Dark and Grimm #2: Jack and Jill escape their own fairy-tale origins and journey through a second string of gruesome Grimm stories.

title: Jackaby
author: William Ritter
description: The first Jackaby novel: a young governess in 1892 New England becomes the assistant to an eccentric investigator who can see the supernatural, and hunts a serial-killing shapeshifter.

title: Beastly Bones
author: William Ritter
description: Jackaby #2: Abigail Rook and R.F. Jackaby investigate a series of bizarre murders in the Connecticut countryside that appear to involve a living dinosaur.

title: Ghostly Echoes
author: William Ritter
description: Jackaby #3: a decade-old murder case returns to haunt Jenny — the resident ghost at Jackaby's — and the Jackaby team must investigate her own death.

title: The Dire King
author: William Ritter
description: Jackaby #4: the final confrontation as the ancient creature known as the Dire King prepares to break the walls between the human and magical worlds.

title: The Pauper Prince and the Eucalyptus Jinn
author: Usman T. Malik
description: Malik's World Fantasy Award-nominated novella: a Pakistani-American physicist investigates his grandfather's claims about a jinn-haunted mango grove in Punjab.

title: Midnight Doorways: Fables from Pakistan
author: Usman T. Malik
description: Malik's first collection: seven literary horror and fantasy stories drawing on Sufi mysticism, Pakistani folklore, and the geopolitical tensions of South Asia.

title: Everything Under
author: Daisy Johnson
description: Johnson's Booker-shortlisted debut: a lexicographer reunites with her estranged mother while returning to the Oxford canals of her childhood and a monster called the Bonak.

title: Sisters
author: Daisy Johnson
description: Two teenage sisters and their mother move to a desolate house on the North York moors after a school incident — and their relationship begins collapsing in uncanny ways.

title: Fen
author: Daisy Johnson
description: Johnson's debut story collection: linked tales of women in the Fenlands of eastern England — haunted, hungering, and thinly separated from the land itself.

title: You
author: Caroline Kepnes
description: Kepnes's debut: a New York bookstore clerk named Joe becomes obsessed with a female customer and narrates his increasingly disturbing stalker campaign in second person.

title: Hidden Bodies
author: Caroline Kepnes
description: You #2: Joe Goldberg has relocated from New York to Los Angeles to escape his past — and has to manage a whole new set of people he cannot help but want to kill.

title: Providence
author: Caroline Kepnes
description: Kepnes's standalone: a teenage boy kidnapped by a Lovecraft-obsessed stranger in Rhode Island emerges years later with an inexplicable and dangerous physical power.

title: You Love Me
author: Caroline Kepnes
description: You #3: Joe has moved to a quiet Pacific Northwest island and fallen for a librarian — but even small islands are not quiet enough to hide Joe's instincts.

title: The Cipher
author: Kathe Koja
description: Koja's debut: two Detroit slackers discover a mysterious black hole in their apartment building's storage room that can reshape any matter placed inside it.

title: Skin
author: Kathe Koja
