description: A performance-art collective in Detroit begins using body modification and metal sculpture as a medium — and the art begins doing things the artists did not intend.

title: Bad Brains
author: Kathe Koja
description: A man recovering from a head injury begins to see a metallic presence in his vision — and finds it is feeding on him.

title: Experimental Film
author: Gemma Files
description: Files's Shirley Jackson Award-winning novel: a Toronto film critic investigating a forgotten Canadian experimental filmmaker discovers the filmmaker's last project was literally dangerous.

title: A Book of Tongues
author: Gemma Files
description: The first Hexslinger novel: a Pinkerton agent infiltrates a gang of supernatural outlaws in the post-Civil War West and is drawn into Aztec blood magic.

title: In the House of the Worm
author: Gemma Files
description: Files's collection of linked cosmic-horror novellas set in a Toronto haunted by a dying cult's lingering god.

title: All the Murmuring Bones
author: A.G. Slatter
description: Slatter's Shirley Jackson-winning gothic: a young woman from a fallen coastal family discovers her ancestors bargained with the merpeople in her grandmother's stories.

title: The Path of Thorns
author: A.G. Slatter
description: Sourdough #2: a governess with a secret family history arrives at a gloomy estate in the Sourdough universe and begins a covert mission of revenge.

title: The Briar Book of the Dead
author: A.G. Slatter
description: Sourdough #3: a young witch in a forest community with its own death-adjacent magic confronts an outside force threatening to destroy everything her people have built.

title: The Gracekeepers
author: Kirsty Logan
description: Logan's debut: in a world mostly covered by water, a floating circus and a lonely island gracekeeper encounter each other and find their fates intertwined.

title: The Gloaming
author: Kirsty Logan
description: On a Scottish island where people turn to stone when they die, a grieving family tries to imagine how they will say goodbye to each other.

title: Things We Say in the Dark
author: Kirsty Logan
description: Logan's story collection: twenty tales of women navigating haunted houses, frozen marriages, and the domestic terrors of everyday Scotland.

title: Nexus
author: Ramez Naam
description: The first Nexus novel: a graduate student develops a drug called Nexus that networks human minds — and attracts the attention of the US Emerging Risks Directorate.

title: Crux
author: Ramez Naam
description: Nexus #2: with Nexus now illegal but still spreading, its creator Kade is hunted by a Buddhist monk who may be the first child born from Nexus minds.

title: Apex
author: Ramez Naam
description: Nexus #3: the conclusion as humanity fractures into factions over the posthuman future — and Nexus becomes the core of a new kind of civilization.

title: Captive Prince
author: C.S. Pacat
description: The first Captive Prince novel: a prince-warrior of one Mediterranean-inspired kingdom is enslaved and given as a gift to the son of his country's invader.

title: Prince's Gambit
author: C.S. Pacat
description: Captive Prince #2: Damen and Laurent's uneasy alliance is tested as they lead Laurent's troops through enemy territory to the border fortress of Charcy.

title: Kings Rising
author: C.S. Pacat
description: Captive Prince #3: the final confrontation as Damen must choose between his own crown and the man he has come to love across lines of war and betrayal.

title: Dark Rise
author: C.S. Pacat
description: The first Dark Rise novel: a young London dockworker discovers he is the reincarnation of an ancient champion in a centuries-spanning war between Light and Dark.

title: Wytches, Vol. 1
author: Scott Snyder
description: Snyder and Jock's folk horror comic: a family moves to a rural New England town to escape tragedy and discovers the woods are full of ancient witches who feed on human flesh.

title: American Vampire, Vol. 1
author: Scott Snyder
description: Snyder and Rafael Albuquerque's Eisner-winning horror comic: a new uniquely American breed of vampire is born in the 1880s West, with the first arc co-written by Stephen King.

title: Batman: The Court of Owls
author: Scott Snyder
description: Snyder and Greg Capullo's New 52 Batman debut: Bruce Wayne discovers a hidden organization, the Court of Owls, has secretly ruled Gotham for centuries.

title: Undiscovered Country, Vol. 1
author: Scott Snyder
description: Snyder and Charles Soule's comic: thirty years after the United States walled itself off from the rest of the world, an international expedition crosses the border.

title: Scalped, Vol. 1: Indian Country
author: Jason Aaron
description: Aaron and R.M. Guera's crime comic: an undercover FBI agent returns to the Lakota reservation he grew up on to bring down the tribal chief running an illegal casino.

title: Southern Bastards, Vol. 1: Here Was a Man
author: Jason Aaron
description: Aaron and Jason Latour's Southern noir: an aging Vietnam vet returns to his corrupt Alabama hometown to clean out his late father's house and walks into a very local war.

title: The Goddamned, Vol. 1: Before the Flood
author: Jason Aaron
description: Aaron and R.M. Guera's Old Testament comic: Cain, the first murderer, has been wandering a pre-Flood Earth for 1,600 years and finally meets a Noah he does not recognize.

title: Thor: God of Thunder, Vol. 1: The God Butcher
author: Jason Aaron
description: Aaron and Esad Ribić's landmark Thor run: three Thors from three different eras unite to fight Gorr, a god who has been systematically murdering gods across the universe.

title: The Vision
author: Tom King
description: King and Gabriel Hernandez Walta's award-winning Marvel mini: the synthetic Avenger Vision builds a family of androids and tries to settle into suburban normalcy.

title: Mister Miracle
author: Tom King
description: King and Mitch Gerads's Eisner-winning mini: the DC escape artist Scott Free attempts suicide, survives, and finds himself fighting a war in heaven while trying to be a dad.

title: The Sheriff of Babylon
author: Tom King
description: King and Mitch Gerads's Iraq war noir: a US ex-cop training police in post-Saddam Baghdad investigates the murder of one of his recruits.

title: Strange Adventures
author: Tom King
description: King, Mitch Gerads, and Doc Shaner's DC mini: Adam Strange returns from his interplanetary adventures to find his memoir being questioned and a war crimes accusation against him.

title: East of West, Vol. 1: The Promise
author: Jonathan Hickman
description: Hickman and Nick Dragotta's alt-history sci-fi western: in a fractured America where the Civil War ended differently, the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse ride again.

title: The Manhattan Projects, Vol. 1: Science, Bad
author: Jonathan Hickman
description: Hickman and Nick Pitarra's alternate-history comic: the Manhattan Project was a front for a much darker WWII research program involving aliens, interdimensional portals, and Einstein's parallel selves.

title: House of X / Powers of X
author: Jonathan Hickman
description: Hickman's relaunch of X-Men: Charles Xavier unifies mutants in a new nation-state on the living island of Krakoa and confronts the mathematical inevitability of mutant extinction.

title: The Black Monday Murders, Vol. 1
author: Jonathan Hickman
description: Hickman and Tomm Coker's occult finance comic: global capitalism is secretly run by four ancient houses of magic, and one of their leaders has just been ritually murdered.

title: The Lives of Tao
author: Wesley Chu
description: The first Tao novel: an out-of-shape Chicago IT guy is unexpectedly chosen as the host for Tao, an ancient alien intelligence whose species has been hiding inside humans for thousands of years.

title: The Deaths of Tao
author: Wesley Chu
description: Tao #2: the two ancient alien factions have grown tired of hiding and are preparing to go to war across Earth in ways humanity cannot comprehend.

title: Time Salvager
author: Wesley Chu
description: A burnt-out time-traveling salvager sent to retrieve resources from Earth's past violates the primary rule of his profession and saves a woman he was never supposed to meet.

title: The Art of Prophecy
author: Wesley Chu
description: The first War Arts Saga novel: the prophesied hero of the Zhuun kingdom is eight years old, already training for his destiny — and the prophecy is wrong.

title: Nightlife
author: Rob Thurman
description: The first Cal Leandros novel: two brothers in New York hide from Cal's inhuman father and his Grendel kin while Cal's monstrous half begins to surface.

title: Moonshine
author: Rob Thurman
description: Cal Leandros #2: Cal and Niko are hired to retrieve a stolen artifact from a werewolf mob and discover the theft is part of a much bigger supernatural power grab.

title: Madhouse
author: Rob Thurman
description: Cal Leandros #3: a serial-killing revenant with a body count in the hundreds of thousands has returned to New York — and is looking for a specific brother to kill next.

title: The Strange Affair of Spring Heeled Jack
author: Mark Hodder
description: The first Burton and Swinburne novel: in an alternate Victorian London, Sir Richard Francis Burton and poet Algernon Swinburne investigate the vigilante called Spring Heeled Jack.

title: The Curious Case of the Clockwork Man
author: Mark Hodder
description: Burton and Swinburne #2: Burton is asked to investigate a strange clockwork creature that has been discovered in Trafalgar Square.

title: Expedition to the Mountains of the Moon
author: Mark Hodder
description: Burton and Swinburne #3: Burton leads a steampunk expedition into East Africa in search of the source of the Nile and an enemy who has been manipulating time itself.

title: Rain Fall
author: Barry Eisler
description: The first John Rain novel: a half-Japanese assassin living in Tokyo who specializes in natural-looking deaths takes a hit on a government official and the case spirals into betrayal.

title: Hard Rain
author: Barry Eisler
description: John Rain #2: Rain is hired to take out a yakuza enforcer and must navigate the tensions between American, Japanese, and Korean intelligence services.

title: Rain Storm
author: Barry Eisler
description: John Rain #3: Rain is forced to work for the CIA on a hit in Macau that turns into a double-cross involving a Mossad agent.

title: The Last Assassin
author: Barry Eisler
description: John Rain #5: Rain returns to Tokyo and discovers he has a son — and that the boy's mother and other Rain enemies are converging.

title: The Woman in Black
author: Susan Hill
description: Hill's classic ghost story: a young solicitor sent to settle the estate of a deceased recluse in an isolated English marsh town begins encountering the figure of a veiled woman in black.

title: The Mist in the Mirror
author: Susan Hill
description: A traveller returning to Victorian England begins to see a ghostly child in mirrors — and to trace a curse that has been following his family for generations.

title: Dolly
author: Susan Hill
description: Hill's short novel: two children spend a summer with their strict aunt in Fenlands England — and one of them brings home a porcelain doll that refuses to be forgotten.

title: The Man in the Picture
author: Susan Hill
description: An Oxford don's favorite painting of a Venetian carnival begins to contain a figure who wasn't there before — and then another, and another.

title: Hawksmoor
author: Peter Ackroyd
description: Ackroyd's Whitbread-winning novel: a contemporary London detective named Hawksmoor investigates a series of murders at the six churches designed by the 18th-century architect Nicholas Dyer — his fictional double.

title: The House of Doctor Dee
author: Peter Ackroyd
description: A contemporary Londoner inherits a house in Clerkenwell that once belonged to the Elizabethan magician John Dee — and finds the 16th-century Dee has been waiting in it.

title: Dan Leno and the Limehouse Golem
author: Peter Ackroyd
description: A series of ritualistic murders in Victorian London is attributed to the 'Limehouse Golem' — and the suspects include Karl Marx, George Gissing, and the music hall star Dan Leno.

title: The Casebook of Victor Frankenstein
author: Peter Ackroyd
description: Ackroyd's Frankenstein reimagined: the young Victor Frankenstein arrives at Oxford in 1816 and befriends Percy Shelley — and his experiments begin in earnest.

title: Monkey Beach
author: Eden Robinson
description: A young Haisla woman from a coastal British Columbia village searches for her missing brother and is guided by the spirits she has been able to see since childhood.

title: Son of a Trickster
author: Eden Robinson
description: The first Trickster Trilogy novel: a troubled Haisla teenager discovers his father is Wee'git the Trickster — and his inheritance is shifting everything around him.

title: Trickster Drift
author: Eden Robinson
description: Trickster Trilogy #2: Jared moves to Vancouver to escape his Trickster heritage and finds that the spirit world has followed him there.

title: The Stain
author: Rikki Ducornet
description: Ducornet's debut novel: a young girl in 19th-century rural France is marked from birth by a strange birthmark and drawn into a Bosch-like world of religious mysticism and cruelty.

title: The Fountains of Neptune
author: Rikki Ducornet
description: Ducornet's fabulist novel: an 80-year-old man in a Brittany fishing village regains his childhood memories during a long coma, and lives inside them.

title: Phosphor in Dreamland
author: Rikki Ducornet
description: A 17th-century man on a fictional Caribbean island invents the first camera — while a half-human, half-avian creature watches from the jungle.

title: Twilight Phantasies
author: Maggie Shayne
description: The first Wings in the Night novel: a Chicago psychiatrist falls for a patient who insists he is a vampire — and slowly realizes he may be telling the truth.

title: Blue Twilight
author: Maggie Shayne
description: Wings in the Night #9: a vampire novelist and a mortal woman with a dark secret are drawn together by an ancient enemy from their past lives.

title: Prince of Twilight
author: Maggie Shayne
description: Wings in the Night #13: a vampire prince locked in an eternal search for his lost bride is finally reunited with her — in a body he does not recognize.

title: Dark Matter
author: Michelle Paver
description: Paver's Arctic ghost story: a 1937 British scientific expedition to Spitsbergen shrinks to a single man who winters alone in the dark — and begins to feel something else in the cabin with him.

title: Thin Air
author: Michelle Paver
description: A 1935 mountaineering expedition to Kangchenjunga discovers something unseen waiting at the base of the death zone.

title: Wakenhyrst
author: Michelle Paver
description: Paver's gothic novel of an Edwardian Suffolk estate where a young woman watches her antiquarian father descend into obsession with a medieval painted panel in the local church.

title: The Court of the Air
author: Stephen Hunt
description: The first Jackelian novel: two orphans in an alternate-Victorian kingdom of Jackals are drawn into a conspiracy involving ancient gods, steam-powered constructs, and the mysterious Court of the Air.

title: The Kingdom Beyond the Waves
author: Stephen Hunt
description: Jackelian #2: a disgraced professor leads an expedition into a hidden ancient civilization — with the Court of the Air and a criminal cartel both pursuing her.

title: The Rise of the Iron Moon
author: Stephen Hunt
description: Jackelian #3: the Kingdom of Jackals faces an invasion from the skies by an alien army that has emerged from the depths of space — and refuses to negotiate.

title: Deadly Class, Vol. 1: Reagan Youth
author: Rick Remender
description: Remender and Wes Craig's comic: a homeless teenager in 1987 San Francisco is recruited to a secret high school for the children of international criminal organizations.

title: Black Science, Vol. 1: How to Fall Forever
author: Rick Remender
description: Remender and Matteo Scalera's comic: an anarchist scientist breaks through the membrane between dimensions and is trapped forever — along with his reluctant team and his children.

title: LOW, Vol. 1: The Delirium of Hope
author: Rick Remender
description: Remender and Greg Tocchini's comic: after humanity has fled to the ocean depths of a dying Earth, the last underwater city receives a probe from the surface — and a reason to hope.

title: Hawkeye, Vol. 1: My Life as a Weapon
author: Matt Fraction
description: Fraction and David Aja's Eisner-winning Marvel run: the Avenger Clint Barton deals with 'what Hawkeye does when he's not being an Avenger' in a grimy NYC apartment building.

title: Sex Criminals, Vol. 1: One Weird Trick
author: Matt Fraction
description: Fraction and Chip Zdarsky's comic: a librarian and a porn actor discover they can both stop time during orgasms — and use the ability to rob banks.

title: Casanova, Vol. 1: Luxuria
author: Matt Fraction
description: Fraction and Gabriel Bá's cult comic: Casanova Quinn, son of a spy-agency director, is kidnapped from his own dimension by a parallel self and recruited into the rival agency.

title: Bitch Planet, Vol. 1: Extraordinary Machine
author: Kelly Sue DeConnick
description: DeConnick and Valentine De Landro's feminist SF: in a distant-future society, women who refuse to comply are sent to an off-world prison called Bitch Planet.

title: Pretty Deadly, Vol. 1: The Shrike
author: Kelly Sue DeConnick
description: DeConnick and Emma Ríos's weird western: Death's daughter Ginny, the Reaper of Vengeance, travels an old-West landscape hunting and being hunted.

title: Captain Marvel: In Pursuit of Flight
author: Kelly Sue DeConnick
description: DeConnick's Carol Danvers relaunch: Carol takes up the mantle of Captain Marvel and reckons with the Kree legacy that gave her her powers.

title: Lazarus, Vol. 1: Family
author: Greg Rucka
description: Rucka and Michael Lark's comic: in a future where sixteen wealthy families rule the world, each family has a 'Lazarus' — a superpowered enforcer who cannot permanently die.

title: Queen & Country, Vol. 1
author: Greg Rucka
description: Rucka's British intelligence comic: a Minder for the Special Operations Section of SIS takes on deniable jobs across the globe — a Bond for a grittier decade.

title: Gotham Central, Vol. 1: In the Line of Duty
author: Greg Rucka
description: Rucka and Ed Brubaker's Batman-adjacent comic: the normal Gotham City police detectives work homicides in a city where superheroes and supervillains are just part of the scene.

title: A Fistful of Rain
author: Greg Rucka
description: Rucka's noir novel: a female roadie for a major rock band goes home to Oregon when her famous rock-star father dies, and finds herself the target of a kidnapper.

title: Secret Six, Vol. 1: Villains United
author: Gail Simone
description: Simone's DC run: a team of six cursed supervillains band together for self-preservation and take on more powerful villains for hire.

title: Birds of Prey, Vol. 1: Of Like Minds
author: Gail Simone
description: Simone's genre-defining Birds of Prey run: Oracle coordinates a team of female DC heroes on operations too delicate for the Justice League.

title: Clean Room, Vol. 1: Immaculate Conception
author: Gail Simone
description: Simone's horror comic: an investigative reporter whose fiancé killed himself after reading a self-help book investigates the mysterious organization behind it.

title: Six Wakes
author: Mur Lafferty
description: A six-person clone crew on a generation ship wake to find that their current bodies are murdered — and the ship's AI has erased the last 25 years of their memories.

title: The Shambling Guide to New York City
author: Mur Lafferty
description: A travel writer in post-recession New York takes a job editing a guidebook for supernatural tourists and discovers the city is full of coterie — zombies, vampires, and other 'coterie.'

title: Solo: A Star Wars Story
author: Mur Lafferty
description: Lafferty's novelization of the Han Solo origin film, expanded with extra scenes and internal monologue.

title: The Shining Girls
author: Lauren Beukes
description: Beukes's breakout novel: a time-traveling serial killer in 1930s Chicago moves through decades hunting 'shining' women — until one of his victims survives and begins hunting him back.

title: Zoo City
author: Lauren Beukes
description: Beukes's Arthur C. Clarke Award-winning novel: in an alternate Johannesburg, criminals are magically bonded to animal familiars as a mark of their guilt — and finding lost people becomes one woman's specialty.

title: Broken Monsters
author: Lauren Beukes
description: A Detroit detective investigates a bizarre murder — a boy's upper body grafted to the lower body of a deer — and discovers a killer trying to make art out of death.

title: Afterland
author: Lauren Beukes
description: A near-future pandemic has killed almost every man on Earth. One mother smuggles her surviving son cross-country, pretending he's a girl, as she is hunted by people who want him for his body.

title: Tower of Silence
author: Sarah Rayne
description: Rayne's psychological thriller: a respected academic becomes obsessed with the truth behind a Tower of Silence — a Zoroastrian death tower — and the woman who vanished from it.

title: A Dark Dividing
author: Sarah Rayne
description: A pair of conjoined twins separated in childhood become the center of a haunting, generations-spanning mystery at an abandoned Shropshire estate.

title: Ghost Song
author: Sarah Rayne
description: A Victorian London music hall reopens for the first time in nearly a century — and the ghosts of its missing performers begin to manifest.

title: The Unfinished World
author: Amber Sparks
description: Sparks's second collection: fourteen strange, lyrical stories of unfinished lives — time travel, ghosts, unlikely sisters, and the moments that never quite happened.

title: And I Do Not Forgive You
author: Amber Sparks
description: Sparks's story collection of contemporary feminist fables: women navigating haunted houses, runaway ghosts, and the occasional apocalypse.

title: The Desert Places
author: Amber Sparks
description: Sparks and Robert Kloss's hybrid novel of prose and illustration: the story of the first human murderer narrated in a high-strange tone by a cosmic observer.

title: Daredevil: Know Fear
author: Chip Zdarsky
description: Zdarsky and Marco Checchetto's Daredevil run: Matt Murdock, recovering from near-death injuries, kills a man in a confrontation and must decide who he really is.

title: Public Domain, Vol. 1
author: Chip Zdarsky
description: Zdarsky's creator-owned comic: a cartoonist whose forgotten superhero character has become the basis of a billion-dollar film franchise finally decides to take his creation back.

title: Afterlift
author: Chip Zdarsky
description: Zdarsky and Jason Loo's graphic novel: a rideshare driver accepts a late-night fare that turns out to be carrying a passenger from one plane of existence to another.

title: Linghun
author: Ai Jiang
description: Jiang's haunting novella: in a small town called HOME, residents can manifest the ghosts of their loved ones — but only as long as they stay.

title: A Palace Near the Wind
author: Ai Jiang
description: Jiang's Ignyte-nominated novella: the first daughter of a wind-clan tribe is promised as a bride to the mechanical king of a rival culture she was raised to fight.

title: I Am AI
author: Ai Jiang
description: Jiang's Hugo-nominated novella: in a near-future where AI has rendered human writing obsolete, a ghostwriter secretly disguised as AI begins to glitch.

title: The Four Profound Weaves
author: R.B. Lemberg
description: Lemberg's novella: two trans elders from rival cultures in the Birdverse travel together to retrieve a sacred woven cloth — and to reclaim their long-delayed lives.

title: Geometries of Belonging
author: R.B. Lemberg
description: Lemberg's collection: linked stories set in the Birdverse exploring trans, non-binary, and neurodivergent characters across a vast mythological fantasy.

title: The Weirdstone of Brisingamen
author: Alan Garner
description: Garner's debut: two modern Cheshire children discover the quartz their mother gave them is an ancient artifact that awakens a sleeping king — and the evil hunting him.

title: The Moon of Gomrath
author: Alan Garner
description: Tales of Alderley #2: the siblings return to the Cheshire countryside and accidentally release the Old Magic — and an ancient entity called the Brollachan.

title: Elidor
author: Alan Garner
description: Four children following a street map in a ruined Manchester neighborhood are drawn into a parallel world called Elidor and are entrusted with its four Treasures.

title: The Owl Service
author: Alan Garner
description: Garner's Carnegie-winning novel: three teenagers at a remote Welsh valley house discover a dinner service with an owl pattern — and unwittingly reenact an ancient Welsh myth.

title: The Weathermonger
author: Peter Dickinson
description: The first Changes Trilogy novel: a 16-year-old boy in a newly medieval England who can control the weather is sent to France — and discovers his own kingdom is under a strange enchantment.

title: The Devil's Children
author: Peter Dickinson
description: Changes Trilogy #3 (chronologically): a young English girl abandoned during the technology-hating backlash is taken in by a group of Sikh refugees and helps them survive.

title: Tulku
author: Peter Dickinson
description: Dickinson's Whitbread-winning novel: during the Boxer Rebellion, a missionary's son flees with a naturalist and is taken into a Tibetan monastery where he may be a reincarnated lama.

title: The Wolves of Willoughby Chase
author: Joan Aiken
description: The first Wolves Chronicles novel: in an alternate-history Victorian England overrun by wolves, two cousins must escape a wicked governess and reclaim their ancestral estate.

title: Black Hearts in Battersea
author: Joan Aiken
description: Wolves Chronicles #2: a young orphan boy arrives in London to study art and is drawn into a Hanoverian plot to overthrow the king.

title: Nightbirds on Nantucket
author: Joan Aiken
description: Wolves Chronicles #3: Dido Twite is rescued at sea and taken to Nantucket by a whaling captain whose daughter has been locked in a tower for years.

title: The Whispering Mountain
author: Joan Aiken
description: A Guardian Award-winning novel loosely connected to the Wolves Chronicles: a Welsh shepherd boy inherits a magical golden harp and must keep it from a wicked Marquess.

title: Five Children and It
author: E. Nesbit
description: Nesbit's classic: five English children on holiday at a sand-pit meet a grumpy Psammead — a sand fairy — who grants them daily wishes they almost always regret.

title: The Phoenix and the Carpet
author: E. Nesbit
description: Psammead Trilogy #2: the same five children find a phoenix egg in their new wishing carpet and are whisked off on a series of adventures.

title: The Story of the Amulet
author: E. Nesbit
description: Psammead Trilogy #3: the children rediscover the Psammead in a Bloomsbury pet shop and use an ancient Egyptian amulet to travel through time.

title: The Enchanted Castle
author: E. Nesbit
description: Three children staying in the English countryside discover a real enchanted castle and a magic ring that grants wishes — and they quickly realize they are not ready.

title: The Thirteen Clocks
author: James Thurber
description: Thurber's fairy tale: a wicked duke has stopped all thirteen clocks in his castle and set an impossible task for any prince who comes to rescue his niece.

title: Fables for Our Time
author: James Thurber
description: Thurber's modern Aesop: short comic fables with moral punchlines, illustrated by the author and widely anthologized in American humor.

title: The Wonderful O
author: James Thurber
description: Thurber's fable: a pirate who hates the letter 'O' invades an island and bans its use, with cascading consequences for the islanders' language and lives.

title: War with the Newts
author: Karel Čapek
description: Čapek's satirical SF novel: the discovery of a species of intelligent giant newts in the Pacific leads to humanity enslaving them — and then to the newts' eventual revolt.

title: R.U.R.
author: Karel Čapek
description: Čapek's landmark play: the company Rossum's Universal Robots manufactures artificial workers — 'robots' — who eventually rise up against their creators. The play that coined the word 'robot.'

title: The Absolute at Large
author: Karel Čapek
description: Čapek's satirical SF: an engineer invents a new kind of power source that releases enormous amounts of energy — and also causes anyone near it to become intensely religious.

title: Natural History
author: Justina Robson
description: Robson's posthuman SF: in a far future where humans are divided between Unevolved and genetically modified Forged, a war is brewing over an artifact that may be the first alien life ever encountered.

title: Living Next Door to the God of Love
author: Justina Robson
description: Robson's transhuman novel: a teenage girl runs away to a city on a gas giant where a god-like being has taken human form — and finds herself slowly becoming a god too.

title: Keeping It Real
author: Justina Robson
description: The first Quantum Gravity novel: a cyborg government agent is assigned to protect a rock-star elf whose concerts are attracting unwanted attention from across the quantum-stabilized realms.

title: The Bone Orchard
author: Sara A. Mueller
description: Mueller's debut: in an imperial courtesan's garden grow living women made of bone and mist — and when the emperor is murdered, the courtesan must use them to solve the case.

title: Journey to the River Sea
author: Eva Ibbotson
description: Ibbotson's Smarties Prize-winning novel: a young orphan is sent to stay with relatives on a rubber plantation in early 20th-century Brazil and falls in love with the Amazon.

title: The Secret of Platform 13
author: Eva Ibbotson
description: Ibbotson's novel (published three years before Harry Potter): a hidden magical platform at King's Cross station leads to an enchanted island, and a prince has been kidnapped from it.

title: Which Witch?
author: Eva Ibbotson
description: Ibbotson's comic fantasy: an eccentric wizard decides to hold a contest to choose a bride, and seven witches of northern England all turn up to compete.

title: Island of the Aunts
author: Eva Ibbotson
description: Three eccentric aunts who secretly run a hidden island kindly inhabited by magical creatures decide to kidnap a child to help them care for the island.

title: The Thirty-Nine Steps
author: John Buchan
description: Buchan's landmark spy thriller: Richard Hannay is framed for a London murder and flees across Scotland while a German espionage cell pursues him.

title: Greenmantle
author: John Buchan
description: Richard Hannay #2: Hannay is sent to Constantinople during World War I to investigate reports of a charismatic prophet called Greenmantle who is inciting a Muslim uprising.

title: Mr. Standfast
author: John Buchan
description: Richard Hannay #3: Hannay infiltrates a pacifist commune in Scotland to root out a German double agent whose betrayal has been killing British soldiers.

title: The Three Hostages
author: John Buchan
description: Richard Hannay #4: Hannay, now a retired Cotswolds squire, is called back into the great game when three prominent young people are kidnapped across Europe.

title: Overlord, Vol. 1: The Undead King
author: Kugane Maruyama
description: The first Overlord light novel: a veteran player of a VR MMO is trapped in the game's closing moments — permanently stuck as his skeletal Overlord character Ainz Ooal Gown.

title: Overlord, Vol. 2: The Dark Warrior
author: Kugane Maruyama
description: Overlord #2: Ainz adopts a human disguise as the adventurer Momon and enters a nearby kingdom to gather information about the world he's now stuck in.

title: Overlord, Vol. 3: The Bloody Valkyrie
author: Kugane Maruyama
description: Overlord #3: Ainz investigates a vampire who has been leaving bodies across the Re-Estize Kingdom — and discovers she is the Bloody Valkyrie Shalltear Bloodfallen, one of his own guardians.

title: I Am Number Four
author: Pittacus Lore
description: The first Lorien Legacies novel: a teenage alien hiding on Earth as a normal high school student learns his pursuers have killed three of the other nine survivors — and Number Four is next.

title: The Power of Six
author: Pittacus Lore
description: Lorien Legacies #2: Number Four is on the run across America while Number Seven tells her own story of growing up in a Spanish convent.

title: The Rise of Nine
author: Pittacus Lore
description: Lorien Legacies #3: as more of the Garde reunite to train together, the Mogadorian invasion begins to move from Earth's margins into major cities.

title: The Fall of Five
author: Pittacus Lore
description: Lorien Legacies #4: the Garde finally find Number Five, but the reunion reveals that not all of them are as unified as they thought.

title: Pym
author: Mat Johnson
description: Johnson's satirical novel: an unemployed Black literature professor obsessed with Edgar Allan Poe's 'The Narrative of Arthur Gordon Pym of Nantucket' organizes an all-Black Antarctic expedition.

title: Loving Day
author: Mat Johnson
description: Johnson's novel: a biracial comic-book artist inherits a dilapidated Philadelphia mansion and meets the daughter he never knew he had — a young woman who identifies as white.

title: Invisible Things
author: Mat Johnson
description: Johnson's SF satire: a scientific expedition to Jupiter discovers a secret colony of abducted humans hidden under a dome — and has to decide whether to rescue them.

title: I'm Thinking of Ending Things
author: Iain Reid
description: Reid's unnerving debut: a young woman accompanies her new boyfriend on a long winter drive to meet his parents at their rural family farm — while thinking about ending their relationship.

title: Foe
author: Iain Reid
description: A couple in a remote farmhouse is visited by a stranger who says the husband has been chosen for a years-long space-exploration mission — and offers a replacement.

title: We Spread
author: Iain Reid
description: An aging woman is moved to a small long-term care home where the staff have unusual plans for her — and where the walls themselves seem unsure.

title: The Will to Believe
author: Iain Reid
description: Reid's newest: a man in a quiet coastal town finds a letter addressed to him from someone who claims to be his future self — and who knows things he has never told anyone.

title: The Book of Nights
author: Sylvie Germain
description: Germain's debut: a French family saga from the Franco-Prussian War to WWII, following the supernatural Péniel family through the century's disasters.

title: Night of Amber
author: Sylvie Germain
description: A sequel to The Book of Nights: the next generation of the Péniel family navigates post-WWII France and the ancestral curse that haunts their blood.

title: The Medusa Child
author: Sylvie Germain
description: A young French girl discovers her brother is a killer — and that her gaze has a power she cannot control. Germain's quiet, devastating short novel.

title: The Watchmaker of Filigree Street
author: Natasha Pulley
description: Pulley's debut: a Home Office clerk in 1883 London receives a mysterious pocket watch that saves his life during a bombing — and meets the Japanese watchmaker who made it.

title: The Bedlam Stacks
author: Natasha Pulley
description: A crippled East India Company smuggler in 1859 Peru is sent to steal cuttings from a cinchona tree — and discovers the village near the tree has been cut off from time.

title: The Lost Future of Pepperharrow
author: Natasha Pulley
description: Watchmaker of Filigree Street #2: the watchmaker Keita Mori returns to Japan with his friend Thaniel, where electricity has been causing unusual phenomena at the new British embassy.

title: The Mars House
author: Natasha Pulley
description: Pulley's SF: a climate refugee from a flooded London becomes the translator for a political assassination target on Mars — and must navigate the planet's unfamiliar gravity and politics.
